---
title: "definition"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=.@"]
---

# definition

```{julia}
module AnimalKingdom
    export Animal, Mammal, Cat, Dog, speak # external interface.

    abstract type Animal end
    abstract type Mammal <: Animal end

    struct Cat <: Mammal
        name::String
        weight::Float64
    end

    struct Dog <: Mammal
        name::String
        breed::String
    end

    speak(a::Animal) = println("Some generic animal sound")
    speak(c::Cat) = println("$(c.name) says meow!")
end

# you could use the types and structs and functions which are exported by the module.
mycat = AnimalKingdom.Cat("FanTuan", 1.0)
mydog = AnimalKingdom.Dog("Buddy", "Golden Retriever")
AnimalKingdom.speak(mycat)
AnimalKingdom.speak(mydog) # the functions are not defined for dog so it will call the generic speak function for Animal.
```
